description_long: This is a standard white mailing envelope with a rectangular shape and a smooth, matte finish. The front side features a printed sender address in the upper left corner, a recipient address in the center, and a United States postage stamp with an American flag in the upper right corner. The back side displays a classic triangular flap, sealed shut, with no visible string-tie or window. The envelope appears to be made of lightweight paper, typical for postal correspondence, and is thin and flat, suggesting it contains a single sheet or a small number of documents. The text and markings are printed in black, and the stamp adds a touch of color with its red, white, and blue design. There are no visible security features or additional embellishments.
description: A white paper mailing envelope with printed addresses and a US flag postage stamp, featuring a sealed triangular flap on the back.
description_view_0: The front view shows the sender and recipient addresses, along with a US flag postage stamp in the upper right corner.
description_view_1: This angled view highlights the thinness of the envelope and the placement of the stamp on the front.
description_view_2: The back view reveals the triangular flap, sealed and unadorned, with no visible openings or windows.
description_view_3: Another angled view from the opposite side, emphasizing the envelope's flatness and the printed details on the front.
attribute_address_markings: Printed sender address (John Smith, 21 Main Street, Mytown, PE C1A 2B3, Canada) and recipient address (Jane Brown, 40 Some Street, Jamestown, NY 12345, USA) are visible.
attribute_presence_of_postage_stamps: One US flag postage stamp is visible in the upper right corner of the front side.
